It's neat - bright, colorful, cool vibes.  I like how you can just relax and smack some lights and cubes around.  Reminds me of playing in the ball pit when I was a kid. Won't keep you entertained for long, but for the price (free) was pretty cool.

I love john
Cozy and Calming little game, very enjoyable.

Really cool to see the orbs bounce off my actual walls and ceilings. This is a great app to demonstrate Mixed Reality to new folks. It’s got a chill vibe of just watching orbs bounce around hitting and breaking boxes. I also like that you can earn additional orbs by watching trailers for the dev’s other games, and by completing 10 levels.

You can push the boxes around with your hands, although you can’t grab them.

When multiple orbs are spawned, sometimes they appear behind the sofa or something. It would be great if there was a way to summon them to your hands.

The app lets you draw in 3D space, but I’m not sure why, and you can’t erase it; so it just sort of clutters your space for a while.

Anyway, this is a very enjoyable experience.

I know I made this, but I have to admit, I play it a lot—not just for testing, but because it's seriously relaxing. Sometimes I just send my orbs flying, and then I flop down on the couch and totally zone out as the musical light show unfolds before me. Even something as simple as clapping to wake up a lazy orb feels oddly satisfying. It’s like a mini-vacation for my brain right in my living room.

reviews.review_description:
The game is amazing it's relaxing fun and a bit challenging, the hand tracking works perfect solving these puzzles with your hands pushing energy orbs and seeing how they not only interact with the cubes but also with your walls and furniture is amazing plus the music the colors is perfect it's one of the best experiences I've had in mixed reality I had a lot of fun playing it in free play mode and completing all the puzzles until I got all the extra orbs and did it all over again I love this game 

About the hint... 
I wanted to complete this game to 100 including the achievements, complete the first three that are easy but the others come out as secrets and I have no idea what I have to do to unlock them, I don't know if you can give me a hint or something to follow because I would really love to complete this game.

Thank you very much

developer_response.body: Hey, thank you for the feedback! There's a hint: you have to be patient and clear many cues ;)

reviews.review_description: Very satisfying, puzzles are a little boring tho.

I'm not entirely sure what the point of Cues is. It's OK to mess around with and I think kids especially will enjoy running around the room chasing after the spheres but it's very repetitive and a bit janky with no overall direction.

Freeplay used the whole room and was going reasonably well until 999 of 1000 blocks had been destroyed and it took a few minutes to realise that the final block I couldn't see anywhere was located inside an area supposedly locked off by a piece of furniture and I had to physically move a chair in order to reach it and finish off the run.

Puzzles took place inside a cube smaller than the room, but the spheres were located outside. Some were out of reach. Once directed into the cube in order to start smashing the puzzle blocks, directing the spheres was pretty hit and miss as they generally don't go the intended direction and they kept 'defying' physics and getting stuck bouncing across the flat surfaces forming the cube walls rather than bouncing off them. Sometimes this was happening at high speed from the initial hit and trying to get a hold of them to reinstate the required movement was honestly tedious and not at all relaxing. Completion was consequently more luck than anything so the puzzle shapes are fairly irrelevant.

For both modes the music produced by the interaction was identical, so in terms of pay off from the efforts put into it all it becomes kind of uninteresting/pointless. To have a more rounded and engaging experience there really needs to be variety in the audio library used to make the music from the interactions.

Overall an easyish way to kill a few minutes but in its current form I can't really see much of a reason to repeat the experience.

A couple of things that might help in future: 

-The aforementioned variety in the audio loops.

-Fix the sphere behaviour to avoid sticking to the cube walls.

-A variable strength 'tractor beam' option to make grabbing and directing the spheres easier, giving the user more control, and the option to sit and chill during freeplay (a potential accessibility benefit).

-A 'half and half' MR option utilising an area with one open wall rather than a fully enclosed cube, allowing the user to freely target the puzzles from outside rather than policing the four walls.

-General variation in design, e.g. different shapes to interact with, to avoid sensory attrition.

Finally, I'm not sure if it's doable but it lends itself to the idea of serving as a kind MR music visualiser which might be a cool way to use it to relax, having the interaction controlled automatically by the user's own audio.

Regardless, I hope it does develop into a more varied and interesting experience in future.
